refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a KPI requirement that must be operationalized with measurable acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target), explicit duty cycle definition, and telemetry-based verification. The refined FR clarifies that cycle time is a system-level performance outcome dependent on vehicle dynamics, operator control responsiveness (including tele-operation latency), and safety constraints that may impose timing overhead.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure p95 cycle time across ≥100 representative load→travel→dump cycles under agreed duty cycle conditions. Correlate with telemetry data (vehicle speed, acceleration, operator input latency, safety system activation events) to isolate performance bottlenecks.
sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within allocated budgets.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall deliver operator commands with latency ≤ latency_budget to preserve cycle time performance.
FR-2.3: The safety services shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding safety_overhead_budget in cycle time impact.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry (start/end timestamps, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety interventions) for KPI verification.

candidate_dps: DP-2a: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/electric actuation response time and travel speed within safety limits; allocate cycle time budgets to load, travel, and dump sub-cycles; implement closed-loop control with feedback.
DP-2b: Minimize teleoperation command latency via low-latency network link (e.g., 5G, dedicated RF) and predictive operator input buffering; implement command queuing and priority scheduling.
DP-2c: Design safety services to execute safety checks in parallel with primary control loops rather than serially; use safety-critical fast-path logic to minimize timing overhead.
DP-2d: Instrument the vehicle and teleoperation system with cycle time telemetry (event timestamps, latency probes, safety event logging); aggregate and report via Telemetry interface.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-2 (Integrated): Achieve cycle time KPI through a coordinated design combining: (1) vehicle actuation optimization with allocated sub-cycle budgets (DP-2a), (2) low-latency teleoperation command delivery (DP-2b), (3) parallel safety constraint enforcement (DP-2c), and (4) comprehensive cycle time telemetry instrumentation (DP-2d). The DP is a system-level orchestration that requires decomposition across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services domains.

potential_sources: Vehicle actuation timing is coupled to teleoperation latency: if operator commands are delayed, vehicle cannot execute within allocated sub-cycle budgets.

Safety constraint enforcement (DP-2c) is coupled to vehicle actuation (DP-2a): safety interventions (e.g., speed limiting, emergency stop) directly impact cycle time; serial safety checks would violate cycle time budget.

Telemetry instrumentation (DP-2d) is coupled to all sub-systems: requires hooks in vehicle control, teleoperation, and safety services to capture timing events; poor instrumentation placement will miss latency sources.
Network link quality (latency, jitter) is a shared resource affecting both teleoperation command delivery and telemetry reporting; congestion in one direction impacts the other.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP exhibits strong coupling across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety domains. This is expected for a system-level KPI requirement. Coupling is not a defect but a signal that decomposition into independent sub-FRs and sub-DPs is necessary to isolate design decisions and enable parallel development.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target and duty cycle are formally defined and agreed with the customer; if not, cycle time optimization is ill-posed.
Assumption: Vehicle actuation hardware (hydraulics, electric motors, transmission) can achieve the required response time; if hardware is legacy/slow, DP-2a may be infeasible.
Assumption: Network link (5G, RF) can deliver latency ≤ latency_budget; if network is congested or unreliable, DP-2b fails.
Assumption: Safety services can be redesigned to execute in parallel without compromising safety integrity; if safety logic is inherently sequential, DP-2c may introduce risk.
Risk: Telemetry overhead (instrumentation, data transmission) may itself consume network bandwidth and introduce latency, creating a feedback loop that degrades cycle time.
Risk: Operator skill and fatigue affect cycle time; tele-operation may introduce variability that inflates p95 beyond p50.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Decompose DP-2 into independent sub-DPs aligned with FR sub-FRs (FR-2.1 through FR-2.4). Establish explicit cycle time budgets for each sub-cycle (load, travel, dump) and allocate latency budgets to teleoperation and safety services. Conduct a feasibility study on network latency and vehicle actuation response time before committing to CT_target.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to deliver the cycle time KPI. At this level, the IM is responsible for orchestrating cycle time performance across all sub-systems, defining and enforcing cycle time budgets, and collecting/reporting telemetry.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution reports cycle time metrics (p95, p50, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety intervention counts) to Site Manager and System Engineer for KPI verification and performance analysis.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives duty cycle definition, CT_target, and operational parameters (e.g., load mass, travel distance, dump height) from Site Manager to configure cycle time optimization.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution reports safety-critical events (e.g., emergency stops, safety constraint violations) that impact cycle time performance to Safety Manager.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution depends on Network Link for teleoperation command delivery (in) and telemetry/safety alert transmission (out). Network latency and reliability directly affect cycle time KPI.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for cycle time KPI orchestration. All sub-systems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are contained within NGHE Solution. Diffusion risk is minimal at this level; the risk is internal decomposition and interface clarity.

Vehicle contains actuation and control logic (DP-2a) but does not own teleoperation latency (DP-2b), safety services (DP-2c), or system-level telemetry aggregation (DP-2d). Assigning the entire DP-2 to Vehicle would diffuse responsibility for cycle time KPI across multiple modules.

Teleoperation owns command latency optimization (DP-2b) but does not own vehicle actuation (DP-2a), safety services (DP-2c), or system-level telemetry (DP-2d). Assigning the entire DP-2 to Teleoperation would fragment cycle time responsibility.

Safety Services owns safety constraint enforcement (DP-2c) but does not own vehicle actuation (DP-2a), teleoperation (DP-2b), or system-level telemetry (DP-2d). Assigning the entire DP-2 to Safety Services would obscure the system-level nature of the KPI.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the correct container for cycle time KPI because: (1) it is the system-level boundary that integrates all sub-systems, (2) it owns the responsibility for defining and enforcing cycle time budgets across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, (3) it owns the telemetry instrumentation and reporting interface, and (4) it can coordinate trade-offs between sub-system performance and safety constraints. Sub-systems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) will own the detailed DPs (DP-2a, DP-2b, DP-2c) and report cycle time performance metrics to NGHE Solution for aggregation.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution may become a 'god module' that owns too many responsibilities if cycle time decomposition is not clear. Mitigation: Establish explicit cycle time budgets and interfaces between NGHE Solution and sub-systems.

Risk: Telemetry instrumentation (DP-2d) may be scattered across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, making it difficult to aggregate cycle time metrics. Mitigation: Define a standard telemetry schema and require all sub-systems to report via the Telemetry interface.

Risk: Network Link is external to NGHE Solution but critical to cycle time performance. If Network Link latency exceeds budget, NGHE Solution cannot meet the KPI. Mitigation: Establish a network latency SLA and include network performance in the duty cycle definition.

Risk: Operator skill and fatigue are external to NGHE Solution but affect cycle time variability. Tele-operation may introduce uncontrollable variance. Mitigation: Define the duty cycle to include operator skill level and fatigue assumptions; measure actual operator performance during acceptance testing.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Assign the refined FR-2 and selected DP-2 to NGHE Solution as the system-level orchestrator. Decompose FR-2 into FR-2.1 through FR-2.4 and assign each to the appropriate sub-system (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, NGHE Solution). Establish explicit cycle time budgets and telemetry reporting requirements for each sub-system. Define the Network Link SLA and operator skill assumptions in the duty cycle.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR-2 is clear and measurable (p95 ≤ CT_target), but the selected DP-2 is a system-level orchestration that exhibits strong coupling across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. The DP is not sufficiently detailed to guide independent design decisions in each sub-system. The DP must be decomposed into independent sub-DPs (DP-2.1, DP-2.2, DP-2.3, DP-2.4) aligned with FR sub-FRs before proceeding to detailed design. Additionally, the DP assumes that CT_target and duty cycle are formally defined; if not, the DP is ill-posed.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: Cycle time telemetry instrumentation (DP-2d) is distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. If each sub-system implements its own telemetry schema, aggregation at NGHE Solution will be complex. Mitigation: Define a standard telemetry schema and require all sub-systems to report via the Telemetry interface.

Diffusion Risk 2: Cycle time budgets are allocated to Vehicle (DP-2a), Teleoperation (DP-2b), and Safety Services (DP-2c), but the allocation logic resides in NGHE Solution. If allocation is not transparent, sub-systems may over-optimize locally and miss system-level targets. Mitigation: Publish cycle time budgets as part of the Tasking interface and require sub-systems to report actual performance via Telemetry.

Diffusion Risk 3: Network Link is external to NGHE Solution but critical to both Teleoperation (DP-2b) and Telemetry (DP-2d). If network latency increases, both command delivery and telemetry reporting degrade. Mitigation: Establish a network latency SLA and include network performance monitoring in the Telemetry interface.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct container for cycle time KPI orchestration. The IM boundary is clear: NGHE Solution owns the system-level responsibility for defining cycle time budgets, coordinating sub-system performance, and reporting telemetry. Sub-systems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) own the detailed DPs and report performance metrics to NGHE Solution. The external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and align with the DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: The refined FR-2 is verifiable via telemetry-based measurement of p95 cycle time across ≥100 representative cycles. Verification requires instrumentation of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to capture timing events and latency probes. The Telemetry interface provides the mechanism for reporting verification data to Site Manager and System Engineer. Verification is feasible if cycle time budgets are allocated and sub-systems report actual performance.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of FR-2 to NGHE Solution is clear: NGHE Solution is responsible for achieving the cycle time KPI by orchestrating Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. The allocation of FR sub-FRs (FR-2.1 through FR-2.4) to sub-systems is clear: Vehicle owns FR-2.1 (sub-cycle timing), Teleoperation owns FR-2.2 (command latency), Safety Services owns FR-2.3 (safety overhead), and NGHE Solution owns FR-2.4 (telemetry). Allocation clarity is high.

recommended_revisions: Revision 1 (FR-DP Gate): Decompose DP-2 into independent sub-DPs (DP-2.1, DP-2.2, DP-2.3, DP-2.4) aligned with FR sub-FRs. Each sub-DP should be sufficiently detailed to guide independent design decisions in the respective sub-system.

Revision 2 (FR-DP Gate): Establish explicit cycle time budgets for each sub-cycle (load, travel, dump) and allocate latency budgets to Teleoperation and safety services. Conduct a feasibility study on network latency and vehicle actuation response time to validate that CT_target is achievable.

Revision 3 (DP-IM Gate): Define a standard telemetry schema for cycle time metrics (start/end timestamps, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety interventions) and require all sub-systems to report via the Telemetry interface.
Revision 4 (DP-IM Gate): Establish a network latency SLA and include network performance monitoring in the Telemetry interface. Define the duty cycle to include network latency assumptions.

Revision 5 (FR-IM Gate): Define the operator skill level and fatigue assumptions in the duty cycle. Measure actual operator performance during acceptance testing to validate that p95 cycle time is achievable with the assumed operator profile.

decision.justification: The refined FR-2 is clear and measurable, and the NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary for cycle time KPI orchestration. However, the selected DP-2 is a system-level orchestration that exhibits strong coupling across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. The DP must be decomposed into independent sub-DPs (DP-2.1, DP-2.2, DP-2.3, DP-2.4) aligned with FR sub-FRs before proceeding to detailed design. Additionally, explicit cycle time budgets must be established and feasibility studies must be conducted to validate that CT_target is achievable. The decision is to ZIGZAG_DOWN to the next decomposition level, where each sub-FR and sub-DP will be assigned to the appropriate sub-system (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services).
next_level_sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within allocated budgets (e.g., load_budget, travel_budget, dump_budget).
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall deliver operator commands with latency ≤ latency_budget to preserve cycle time performance.
FR-2.3: The safety services shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding safety_overhead_budget in cycle time impact.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry (start/end timestamps, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety interventions) for KPI verification.

revise.why: The selected DP-2 is a system-level orchestration that must be decomposed into independent sub-DPs aligned with FR sub-FRs. Each sub-DP should be sufficiently detailed to guide independent design decisions in the respective sub-system (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). Additionally, explicit cycle time budgets must be established and feasibility studies must be conducted to validate that CT_target is achievable.

assumptions: Assumption-1: CT_target and duty cycle are formally defined and agreed with the customer.
Assumption-2: Vehicle actuation hardware (hydraulics, electric motors, transmission) can achieve the required response time.
Assumption-3: Network link (5G, RF) can deliver latency ≤ latency_budget.
Assumption-4: Safety services can be redesigned to execute in parallel without compromising safety integrity.
Assumption-5: Operator skill level and fatigue are consistent with the duty cycle assumptions.
Assumption-6: Telemetry instrumentation overhead is negligible and does not degrade cycle time performance.
open_risks: Risk-1: If CT_target is not achievable with the current vehicle actuation hardware, the KPI requirement is infeasible.
Risk-2: If network latency exceeds latency_budget, teleoperation command delivery will be delayed and cycle time will increase.
Risk-3: If safety services cannot be redesigned to execute in parallel, safety overhead will exceed safety_overhead_budget.
Risk-4: If telemetry instrumentation is scattered across sub-systems, aggregation at NGHE Solution will be complex and error-prone.
Risk-5: If operator skill and fatigue are not controlled, cycle time variability (p95 - p50) may be large and uncontrollable.
Risk-6: If network link is congested, both teleoperation command delivery and telemetry reporting will degrade, creating a feedback loop.
evidence_needed: Evidence-1: Formal definition of CT_target and duty cycle (load mass, travel distance, dump height, operator skill level, site conditions).
Evidence-2: Feasibility study on vehicle actuation response time and travel speed under the agreed duty cycle.
Evidence-3: Network latency measurement and SLA definition for teleoperation and telemetry.
Evidence-4: Safety services design review to confirm that parallel execution is feasible without compromising safety integrity.
Evidence-5: Telemetry schema definition and instrumentation plan for Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.
Evidence-6: Operator skill profile and fatigue model for the duty cycle; acceptance test plan to measure actual operator performance.
